content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN vertices.
Vertex iii has the integer AiA_iAi​ written on it. It is guaranteed that Ai≠0A_i \ne 0Ai​=0.

A walk in the tree is a sequence of vertices (x1,x2,…,xk)(x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_k)(x1​,x2​,…,xk​) such that there exists an edge between xix_ixi​ and xi+1x_{i+1}xi+1​ for each 1≤i<k1 \le i \lt k1≤i<k.

The length of such a walk is said to be kkk.
Note that in particular, it is allowed for vertices to repeat in a walk.

The score of a walk is defined as follows:


Start with P=1P = 1P=1.
Then, for each i=1,2,3,…,ki = 1, 2, 3, \ldots, ki=1,2,3,…,k in order, update PPP using the following rules:

If vertex xix_ixi​ has appeared before in the walk, do nothing.

That is, each vertex must only be processed the first time it's seen.
Otherwise, let w=Axiw = A_{x_i}w=Axi​​ be the value at the current vertex. Then,

If w>0w \gt 0w>0, multiply PPP by 2w2^{w}2w.
If w<0w \lt 0w<0, multiply PPP by −2−w-2^{-w}−2−w.




The score of the walk is then the final value of PPP, after all kkk vertices have been processed.
Your task is to compute the maximum possible score of a walk in the tree. The walk can start and end at any vertex, and have any positive length (in particular it's allowed to start and end at the same vertex without moving).

The answer can be large, so report it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the number of vertices.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the values written on the vertices.
The next N−1N - 1N−1 lines describe the edges. The iii-th of these N−1N - 1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer: the maximum possible score of a walk in the graph, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The input graph is a tree on {1,2,…,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,…,N}.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: One optimal walk is (1,2,3)(1, 2, 3)(1,2,3), which starts at 111 and ends at 333, moving through 222.
The score of this walk is as follows:

Start with P=1P = 1P=1.
First vertex is 111. A1=−1<0A_1 = -1 \lt 0A1​=−1<0, so we multiply −2−A1=−2-2^{-A_1} = -2−2−A1​=−2 to PPP.
Now, P=−2P = -2P=−2.
Second vertex is 222. A2=−1<0A_2 = -1 \lt 0A2​=−1<0, so we multiply −2−A2=−2-2^{-A_2} = -2−2−A2​=−2 to PPP.
Now, P=4P = 4P=4.
Third vertex is 333. A3=2>0A_3 = 2 \gt 0A3​=2>0, so we multiply 2A3=22=42^{A_3} = 2^2 = 42A3​=22=4 to PPP.
Now, P=16P = 16P=16.

It can be proved that this is optimal.
Test case 222: One optimal walk is (1)(1)(1), i.e. to start at 111 and just not move.
Since A1>0A_1 \gt 0A1​>0, the score of this walk is 2A1=25=322^{A_1} = 2^5 = 322A1​=25=32.
